identity.short_name: Mixed LTE count with an order-2 bridge and exact depths

problem_spec.summary: Two-prime counting problem where one prime uses a difference-of-powers valuation and the other uses a sum-of-powers valuation; order data forces the relevant base congruences and exact p-adic depths are enforced by excluding the unique Hensel lift at the prime-square layer.
ask: Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of residue classes $x\pmod M$ such that $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$, $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$, and simultaneously $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$. Determine $|S|$ and output the unique integer $0\le N<1000$ with $N\equiv |S|\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime at which a difference-of-powers LTE constraint is imposed

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: prime at which a sum-of-powers LTE constraint is imposed

parameter(s).name: A
parameter(s).purpose: target exact depth $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and modulus exponent $p^{A+1}$

parameter(s).name: B
parameter(s).purpose: target exact depth $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ and modulus exponent $q^{B+1}$

parameter(s).name: d_p
parameter(s).purpose: prescribed nontrivial order modulo $p$; keeps $v_q(n/d_q)$ sharp

parameter(s).name: d_q
parameter(s).purpose: half the prescribed even order modulo $q$ (order $2d_q$); keeps $v_p(n/d_p)$ sharp

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: structured exponent coupling the order constraints to exact valuation depths, and (via the parity guard) enabling LTE for a sum of powers

parameter(s).name: M
parameter(s).purpose: structured two-prime modulus for independent prime-power lifting and CRT recombination

invariant(s).name: order2_bridge_to_negative_one
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ then $x^{d_q}$ has order $2$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ and hence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$, so $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$.

invariant(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
invariant(s).statement: The guard that $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd implies $n/d_q$ is odd (since $p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ is odd), enabling the odd-exponent LTE formula for $v_q(x^n+1)$.

invariant(s).name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
invariant(s).statement: Under $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$, one has $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$.

invariant(s).name: mixed_order_to_LTE_bridge
invariant(s).statement: For admissible $x$, $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$ gives $p\mid(x^{d_p}-1)$ and yields (LTE) $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$; and $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ gives $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$ and (with $n/d_q$ odd) yields $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$.

invariant(s).name: exact_depth_equivalence
invariant(s).statement: Using the structured exponent valuations, the targets $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ are equivalent to $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$.

essential_assumption(s): All primes are odd; no 2-adic exceptional LTE branches occur.
Cross-coprimality guards $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$ ensure $v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(n/d_q)$ are exactly $A-1$ and $B-1$ with no extra prime factors leaking in from $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)$.
Parity guard: $n/d_q$ is odd (equivalently $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ odd), required for the sum-of-powers LTE identity at $q$.
The order constraint at $q$ is even ($\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$) so that $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$; without this, $v_q(x^n+1)$ would not be controlled by LTE in the intended way.
uniqueness_certificate(s): For each admissible parameter tuple, the constraints reduce (via LTE bridges) to exact valuation conditions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$. For each residue of order $d_p$ modulo $p$ and each residue of order $2d_q$ modulo $q$, Hensel's lemma for simple roots implies there is exactly one lift to the prime-square layer with valuation $\ge 2$ for the relevant polynomial ($y^{d_p}-1$ at $p$, $y^{d_q}+1$ at $q$), hence exactly $p-1$ (resp. $q-1$) admissible lifts with valuation $1$. All higher lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$ are free and contribute a factor $r^{A_r-1}$. The $p$- and $q$-conditions are independent and recombine uniquely by CRT, so $|S|$ (hence $|S|\bmod 1000$) is uniquely determined.

technique(s).name: order_count_in_cyclic_group_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: Count residues of exact order $d_p$ modulo $p$ and exact order $2d_q$ modulo $q$ using cyclicity of unit groups modulo primes.

technique(s).name: order2_to_negative_one_bridge
technique(s).purpose: Convert an even order condition into the congruence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ needed for sum-of-powers LTE.

technique(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
technique(s).purpose: Ensure $n/d_q$ is odd so the odd-exponent LTE sum formula applies.

technique(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers
technique(s).purpose: Compute $v_p(x^n-1)$ exactly after order reduction to a difference of powers.

technique(s).name: LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
technique(s).purpose: Compute $v_q(x^n+1)$ exactly after order reduction to a sum of powers.

technique(s).name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
technique(s).purpose: Extract the fixed values $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$ under the cross-coprimality guards.

technique(s).name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
technique(s).purpose: Turn target depths $A,B$ into prime-square exclusion statements on $x^{d_p}-1$ and $x^{d_q}+1$.

technique(s).name: hensel_filter_for_exact_depth
inputs: polynomials $y^{d_p}-1$ and $y^{d_q}+1$ with simple roots modulo primes
technique(s).purpose: Show that among the $r$ lifts of a fixed root modulo $r$ to modulo $r^2$, exactly one lift makes the polynomial divisible by $r^2$; excluding it forces valuation exactly $1$.
outputs: exactly $p-1$ good lifts at the $p^2$ layer per order-$d_p$ residue
exactly $q-1$ good lifts at the $q^2$ layer per order-$2d_q$ residue

technique(s).name: mod_prime_power_lifting_count
technique(s).purpose: Count free lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$ and ensure the valuation decision persists under lifting.

technique(s).name: CRT_product_count
technique(s).purpose: Recombine prime-power solutions into solutions modulo $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ via CRT bijection.

theorem(s).name: cyclicity_of_units_mod_odd_prime
theorem(s).role: $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic; enables exact-order counting via Euler totient.

theorem(s).name: unique_element_of_order_2_in_cyclic_group
theorem(s).role: A cyclic group has a unique element of order $2$; in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ it is $-1$, giving $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ when $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$.

theorem(s).name: vp_sum_lower_bound
theorem(s).role: Valuation bookkeeping used inside LTE arguments to isolate the first nonvanishing term.

theorem(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers_for_odd_prime
theorem(s).role: If odd prime $r\mid(a-b)$ and $r\nmid ab$, then $v_r(a^m-b^m)=v_r(a-b)+v_r(m)$.

theorem(s).name: LTE_sum_of_powers_for_odd_prime_odd_exponent
theorem(s).role: If odd prime $r\mid(a+b)$, $r\nmid ab$, and $m$ is odd, then $v_r(a^m+b^m)=v_r(a+b)+v_r(m)$.

theorem(s).name: hensel_unique_lift_for_simple_roots
theorem(s).role: Simple roots of a polynomial modulo an odd prime lift uniquely to roots modulo the prime square; used for both $y^{d_p}-1$ and $y^{d_q}+1$.

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: Provides a bijection between residues modulo a product of coprime moduli and pairs of residues modulo each factor.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Translate multiplicative-order information into divisibility of $x^d\pm 1$, then use LTE (difference and sum) to obtain exact p-adic depths and count solutions by lifting across prime powers.

critical_trick(s): Use the even order condition $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ to force $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ (unique order-2 element) and combine with a parity gate so that LTE applies to the sum $x^n+1$.
Apply LTE separately to $x^n-1$ at $p$ and to $x^n+1$ at $q$ to convert target depths into the prime-square exclusions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$.
Count by excluding the unique Hensel lift at each prime-square layer and then freely lifting to higher prime powers, and recombine counts via CRT.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Integer arithmetic (lcm, modular exponentiation, gcd, valuation loops).

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Canonicalization of kernel polynomials for the novelty equivalence audit.

library(s).name: random
library(s).reason: Randomized stress tests comparing direct valuations with the LTE-predicted identities.

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness checks aligned to the intended structure:
- Enumerated all residues modulo $11^2$ and counted those whose reduction mod $11$ has order $5$ and for which $11\mid(x^5-1)$ but $11^2\nmid(x^5-1)$; obtained $40$ and lifted by a factor $11^3$ to get $N_{11}=53240$.
- Enumerated all residues modulo $19^2$ and counted those whose reduction mod $19$ has order $18$ and for which $19\mid(x^9+1)$ but $19^2\nmid(x^9+1)$; obtained $108$ and lifted by a factor $19^3$ to get $N_{19}=740772$.
- Multiplied the independent prime-power counts (CRT interface) to get $|S|=39438701280$, hence $|S|\bmod 1000=280$.
Preflight attempts:
Attempt A: Naive heuristic (ignore exact-depth filters and only count order conditions with arbitrary lifts) gives $\varphi(5)\,11^4\cdot\varphi(18)\,19^4\equiv 264\pmod{1000}$, which differs from $280$.
Attempt B: Bounded scan over $x\in\{1,2,\dots,200000\}$ viewed as distinct residues modulo $M$ found $19785$ hits, i.e. $785\bmod 1000$, not $280$.
Attempt C: Partial information (e.g. only knowing $|S|\equiv 30\pmod{125}$) leaves $8$ candidates in $\{0,\dots,999\}$ (namely $30,155,280,405,530,655,780,905$), so it cannot determine the output without the full structure.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason:
Independent verification via valuation identities:
- "Random stress test on residues modulo $11^5$: for sampled $x$ satisfying the order-$5$ condition mod $11$, verified directly (by checking congruences mod $11^k$) that $v_{11}(x^n-1)=v_{11}(x^5-1)+v_{11}(n/5)$."
- "Random stress test on residues modulo $19^5$: for sampled $x$ satisfying the order-$18$ condition mod $19$, verified directly that $v_{19}(x^n+1)=v_{19}(x^9+1)+v_{19}(n/9)$ (using the odd-exponent sum LTE regime)."
- "Verified the \"unique bad lift property computationally: for each order-$5$ residue mod $11$, among its $11$ lifts to mod $11^2$ exactly one satisfies $y^5\\equiv 1\\pmod{11^2}$; for each order-$18$ residue mod $19$, among its $19$ lifts to mod $19^2$ exactly one satisfies $y^9\\equiv -1\\pmod{19^2}$."

task(s).name: novelty_equivalence_audit
task(s).reason:
Canonicalized the parent and daughter kernel polynomials in sympy by expand+together+cancel+factor and monic normalization, and attempted equivalence under a deterministic symmetry set:
- swap the two prime components (p<->q),
- substitute $z\mapsto -z$,
- substitute $z\mapsto 1/z$ with denominator clearing,
- substitute $z\mapsto -1/z$ with denominator clearing.
No transform made the parent kernel multiset match the daughter's (the daughter includes a sum-of-powers constraint $z^{d_q}+1$ whereas the parent uses only $z^d-1$ constraints).
